intro: Lebanon (meaning white, from the snowfields visible on its peaks much of the year) is the great mountain range of ancient Syria running north-south parallel to the Mediterranean coast. The Lebanon range proper and the Anti-Lebanon (Hermon range) enclose the fertile Beqaa valley between them. Lebanon marked the northern extent of the territory promised to Israel (Deut. 1:7; Josh. 1:4) and was celebrated for its magnificent cedar forests, which furnished timber for Solomon's temple (1 Kgs. 5:6) and for Hiram's commerce. The "glory of Lebanon" — its towering cedars — became a biblical symbol of greatness and divine blessing (Isa. 35:2; 60:13). The Song of Solomon uses Lebanon repeatedly as a landscape of beauty, fragrance, and strength (Song 3:9; 4:8, 11, 15).
